article_title: CISA Adds Windows Shell and ConnectWise ScreenConnect Flaws to KEV After Microsoft's April Patch Failed to Mark Zero-Click Bug as Exploited

content_preview.title: CISA Adds Windows Shell and ConnectWise ScreenConnect Flaws to KEV After Microsoft's April Patch Failed to Mark Zero-Click Bug as Exploited
content_preview.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-32202 and CVE-2024-1708 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on April 28, giving federal agencies until May 12 to patch a zero-click NTLM coercion flaw whose Patch Tuesday entry carried no exploitation marker.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added two actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on April 28, 2026, ordering Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies to patch them by May 12. The two entries — a zero-click Windows Shell flaw tracked as [CVE-2026-32202](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-32202) and a two-year-old ConnectWise ScreenConnect path traversal bug tracked as CVE-2024-1708 — were added together in ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong cybersecurity News piece (886 words). Primary-sourced via CISA alert + NVD CVE entry + SecurityWeek's Akamai-attributed reporting. The bot followed the project memory pattern and used CISA + NVD as direct primary sources.

source_verification.https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/: source-0.html — CISA alert verifies: April 28 KEV addition of CVE-2026-32202 (Windows Shell Protection Mechanism Failure) and CVE-2024-1708 (ConnectWise ScreenConnect Path Traversal), BOD 22-01 framing, 'strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure'. The alert page itself does not include the May 12 deadline — the deadline is in the catalog JSON.
source_verification.live KEV catalog (verified via WebFetch: Confirmed: CVE-2026-32202 dateAdded 2026-04-28 / dueDate 2026-05-12; CVE-2024-1708 dateAdded 2026-04-28 / dueDate 2026-05-12. The article's May 12 deadline claim is verbatim accurate.
source_verification.https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-202: source-1.html — verified verbatim: CVSS 4.3 MEDIUM, CWE-693 Protection Mechanism Failure, 'Protection mechanism failure in Windows Shell allows an unauthorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network', Windows 10 22H2, Windows 11 23H2/26H1, Windows Server 2012 R2 affected.
source_verification.https://www.securityweek.com/incomplete-: source-2.html — verified verbatim: Akamai researchers reported the gap, 'the victim machine was still authenticating to the attacker's server' quote, 'triggers an automatic NTLM authentication handshake, sending the victim's Net-NTLMv2 hash to the attacker, which can later be used for NTLM relay attacks' quote, APT28/Fancy Bear attribution to original CVE-2026-21510, December 2025 attacks against Ukraine and EU countries, chaining with CVE-2026-21513.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified verbatim across primary (CISA + KEV JSON + NVD) and secondary (SecurityWeek/Akamai) sources. The internal cross-reference to the April 15 Patch Tuesday article (163 fixes) checks out — that article exists in the archive.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. The 'missing exploitation flag' framing is editorial but defensible — the gap between Microsoft's release notes and the KEV catalog is real and meaningful for enterprise patch triage.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from yesterday's Marimo CVE article. Different threat (zero-click NTLM coercion vs RCE), different ecosystem (Windows + ConnectWise vs Python notebook), different angle (incomplete patch / missing exploited flag).
recommendations: The May 12 deadline came from the KEV JSON feed rather than the alert HTML page — the bot cited cisa.gov correctly but readers who follow the link to the alert page won't find the deadline there. Future cybersecurity articles citing KEV deadlines could optionally add the JSON feed URL or a direct catalog deeplink. Non-blocking.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Primary-source verified end-to-end. The bot followed the new memory pattern (CISA KEV + NVD as direct primary sources) and produced a well-sourced piece.
